title: Cyclophyllum pancheri
intro: Cyclophyllum pancheri, also known as Pancheri's wood-vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in New Caledonia, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyclophyllum pancheri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment, and is used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and wounds.
taxonomy: Cyclophyllum pancheri is classified within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. The genus Cyclophyllum is part of the Apocynales order, although some sources place it in the Gentianales order. The classification can vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a member of the Rubiaceae family. The exact classification can be complex due to the ongoing revisions in plant taxonomy.
uses: Cyclophyllum pancheri is a plant species native to the Pacific Islands, and it has been traditionally used in local medicine for various purposes. The plant's extracts and oils are believed to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which can be used to treat minor wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. In traditional medicine, the plant is also used to treat fever, headaches, and digestive problems. Additionally, the plant's leaves and bark are used in rituals and ceremonies to promote spiritual healing and protection. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Cyclophyllum pancheri in modern medicine.
